revision.text:
{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōshōji Kofun | native_name =光正寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koshoji Kofun03.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|41|N|130|29|59|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Umi, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middle of the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[Haji ware]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Koshoji Kofun}} *[https://www.town.umi.lg.jp/site/spot/kanko131.html Umi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11548 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=19 Cultural Porperties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōshōji Kofun | native_name =光正寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koshoji Kofun03.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|41|N|130|29|59|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Umi, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middle of the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[Haji ware]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Koshoji Kofun}} *[https://www.town.umi.lg.jp/site/spot/kanko131.html Umi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11548 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=19 Cultural Porperties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōshōji Kofun | native_name =光正寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koshoji Kofun03.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|41|N|130|29|59|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Umi, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middle of the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[Haji ware]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Koshoji Kofun}} *[https://www.town.umi.lg.jp/site/spot/kanko131.html Umi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11548 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=19 Cultural Porperties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middle[[Category:middle]] of the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[Haji ware]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middle[[Category:middle]] of the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[Haji pottery]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Koshoji Kofun]][[en:Kōshōji Kofun]][[ja:光正寺古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q84128643]] Wikidata name: Kōshōji Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|image = File:Koshoji Kofun03.jpg|location = {{ill|Umi|qid=Q945518}}|coordinates = {{Coord|33.57791666666667|N|130.4998611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōshōji Kofun | native_name =光正寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koshoji Kofun03.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|41|N|130|29|59|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[:en:Hakata Bay|Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[:en:talc|talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[:en:Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[:en:Dongyi|Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[:en:Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kashii Line|Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Koshoji Kofun}} *[https://www.town.umi.lg.jp/site/spot/kanko131.html Umi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11548 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=19 Cultural Porperties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[:en:Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[:en:Dongyi|Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[:en:Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kashii Line|Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Koshoji Kofun]][[en:Kōshōji Kofun]][[ja:光正寺古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q84128643]] Wikidata name: Kōshōji Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|image = File:Koshoji Kofun03.jpg|location = {{ill|Umi|WD=Q945518}}|coordinates = {{Coord|33.57791666666667|N|130.4998611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōshōji Kofun | native_name =光正寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koshoji Kofun03.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|41|N|130|29|59|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[:en:Hakata Bay|Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[:en:talc|talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[:en:Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[:en:Dongyi|Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[:en:Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kashii Line|Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Koshoji Kofun]][[en:Kōshōji Kofun]][[ja:光正寺古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q84128643]] Wikidata name: Kōshōji Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|image = File:Koshoji Kofun03.jpg|location = {{ill|Umi|WD=Q945518}}|coordinates = {{Coord|33.57791666666667|N|130.4998611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōshōji Kofun | native_name =光正寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koshoji Kofun03.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|41|N|130|29|59|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[:en:Hakata Bay|Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[:en:talc|talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[:en:Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[:en:Dongyi|Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[:en:Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kashii Line|Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[:en:Hakata Bay|Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[:en:talc|talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[:en:Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[:en:Dongyi|Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[:en:Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kashii Line|Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[:en:Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[:en:Dongyi|Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[:en:Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kashii Line|Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[:en:Hakata Bay|Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[:en:talc|talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[:en:Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[:en:Dongyi|Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[:en:Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kashii Line|Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[:en:Hakata Bay|Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[:en:talc|talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[:en:Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[:en:Dongyi|Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[:en:Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kashii Line|Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Umi, Fukuoka|Umi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun |zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into [[:en:Hakata Bay|Hakata Bay]] and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including [[:en:Basalt|Basalt]] thought to be from Noko Island and [[:en:talc|talc]] from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to [[:en:Records of the Three Kingdoms|The History of the Three Kingdoms]], [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]]'s [[:en:Dongyi|Dongyi]] biography, commonly known as [[:en:Wajinden|Wajin Biography]], and [[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]].
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kashii Line|Kashii Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Umi, Fukuoka|en|Umi, Fukuoka|WD=Q945518|lt=Umi}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun |en|Zenpokoenfun |WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|WD=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay}} and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including {{ill|Basalt|en|Basalt|WD=Q43338|lt=Basalt}} thought to be from Noko Island and {{ill|talc|en|talc|WD=Q134583|lt=talc}} from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|en|Records of the Three Kingdoms|WD=Q247318|lt=The History of the Three Kingdoms}}, {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei}}'s {{ill|Dongyi|en|Dongyi|WD=Q333910|lt=Dongyi}} biography, commonly known as {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajin Biography}}, and {{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties}}.
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kashii Line|en|Kashii Line|WD=Q5359548|lt=Kashii Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Koshoji Kofun}} *[https://www.town.umi.lg.jp/site/spot/kanko131.html Umi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11548 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=19 Cultural Porperties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|41|N|130|29|59|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Umi, Fukuoka|en|Umi, Fukuoka|WD=Q945518|lt=Umi, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Umi, Fukuoka|en|Umi, Fukuoka|WD=Q945518|lt=Umi}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun |en|Zenpokoenfun |WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|WD=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay}} and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including {{ill|Basalt|en|Basalt|WD=Q43338|lt=Basalt}} thought to be from Noko Island and {{ill|talc|en|talc|WD=Q134583|lt=talc}} from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|en|Records of the Three Kingdoms|WD=Q247318|lt=The History of the Three Kingdoms}}, {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei}}'s {{ill|Dongyi|en|Dongyi|WD=Q333910|lt=Dongyi}} biography, commonly known as {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajin Biography}}, and {{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties}}.
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kashii Line|en|Kashii Line|WD=Q5359548|lt=Kashii Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Koshoji Kofun}} *[https://www.town.umi.lg.jp/site/spot/kanko131.html Umi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11548 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=19 Cultural Porperties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}|image = File:Koshoji Kofun03.jpg|location = {{ill|Umi|WD=Q945518}}|coordinates = {{Coord|33.57791666666667|N|130.4998611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Kōshōji Kofun | native_name =光正寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koshoji Kofun03.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|41|N|130|29|59|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Umi, Fukuoka|en|Umi, Fukuoka|WD=Q945518|lt=Umi, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Umi, Fukuoka|en|Umi, Fukuoka|WD=Q945518|lt=Umi}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun |en|Zenpokoenfun |WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|WD=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay}} and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including {{ill|Basalt|en|Basalt|WD=Q43338|lt=Basalt}} thought to be from Noko Island and {{ill|talc|en|talc|WD=Q134583|lt=talc}} from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|en|Records of the Three Kingdoms|WD=Q247318|lt=The History of the Three Kingdoms}}, {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei}}'s {{ill|Dongyi|en|Dongyi|WD=Q333910|lt=Dongyi}} biography, commonly known as {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajin Biography}}, and {{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties}}.
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kashii Line|en|Kashii Line|WD=Q5359548|lt=Kashii Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Koshoji Kofun}} *[https://www.town.umi.lg.jp/site/spot/kanko131.html Umi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11548 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=19 Cultural Porperties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koshoji Kofun]][[en:Kōshōji Kofun]][[ja:光正寺古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koshoji Kofun]][[en:Kōshōji Kofun]][[ja:光正寺古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Koshoji Kofun]][[en:Kōshōji Kofun]][[ja:光正寺古墳]]
{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}|image = File:Koshoji Kofun03.jpg|location = {{ill|Umi|WD=Q945518|1=Umi, Fukuoka}}|coordinates = {{Coord|33.57791666666667|N|130.4998611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Kōshōji Kofun | native_name =光正寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Koshoji Kofun03.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōshōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|41|N|130|29|59|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Umi, Fukuoka|en|Umi, Fukuoka|WD=Q945518|lt=Umi, Fukuoka|1=Umi, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|44|N|130|47|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōshōji Kofun'''|光正寺古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kōshōji neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Umi, Fukuoka|en|Umi, Fukuoka|WD=Q945518|lt=Umi|1=Umi, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191972|title=光正寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōshōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun |en|Zenpokoenfun |WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is built on a hill on the right bank of the Umi River, which flows into {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|WD=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay|1=Hakata Bay}} and is orientated to the west-northwest. It has a total length is approximately 54 meters, with a posterior circular portion 34 meters in diameter and five meters high. The anterior rectangular portion is approximately 20 meters wide and three meters high. The upper portion of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]''. It is estimated from its shape and from excavated pottery shards to have been constructed in the first half of the Kofun period, or around the middleof the third century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular portion was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing three box-style sarcophagi, one wooden coffin, and one earthenware coffin. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included ''[[magatama]]'' beads, cylindrical beads, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery pots, and iron swords. The largest box-shaped sarcophagus was made from stones brought from a great distance, including {{ill|Basalt|en|Basalt|WD=Q43338|lt=Basalt|1=Basalt}} thought to be from Noko Island and {{ill|talc|en|talc|WD=Q134583|lt=talc|1=Talc}} from Mt. Wakasugi. The smaller sarcophagi are made from local stone. These three sarcophagi were also lined with vermillion pigment.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Kōshōji Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in Kasuya District, and is thought to be the tomb of king of {{Nihongo|'''Fumikoku'''|不弥国}}, one of the countries that existed in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in the {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=不弥国(fumikoku) - 邪馬台国 |url=http://nihon-rekishi.com/yamataikoku/fumikoku.html |access-date=2022-06-26 |website=nihon-rekishi.com}}</ref>according to {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|en|Records of the Three Kingdoms|WD=Q247318|lt=The History of the Three Kingdoms|1=Records of the Three Kingdoms}}, {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei|1=Cao Wei}}'s {{ill|Dongyi|en|Dongyi|WD=Q333910|lt=Dongyi|1=Dongyi}} biography, commonly known as {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajin Biography|1=Wajinden}}, and {{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties|1=History of the Northern Dynasties}}.
Artifacts from the site are stored in the Umi Town Museum of History and Folklore. Currently, the surrounding area to the tumulus is being maintained as a historical park, and a one-fifth scale model of the tumulus as it originally looked is located in the Guidance Plaza. The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sue-Chuo Station|Sue-Chuo Station]] on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kashii Line|en|Kashii Line|WD=Q5359548|lt=Kashii Line|1=Kashii Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
